elements.sent-2411-001: We explore solutions for fairly allocating indivisible items among agents assigned weights representing their entitlements.
elements.sent-2411-002: Our fairness goal is weighted-envy-freeness (WEF), where each agent deems their allocated portion relative to their entitlement at least as favorable as any other’s relative to their own.
elements.sent-2411-003: In many cases, achieving WEF necessitates monetary transfers, which can be modeled as third-party subsidies.
elements.sent-2411-004: The goal is to attain WEF with bounded subsidies.
elements.sent-2411-005: Previous work in the unweighted setting of subsidies relied on basic characterizations of EF that fail in the weighted settings.
elements.sent-2411-006: This makes our new setting challenging and theoretically intriguing.
elements.sent-2411-007: We present polynomial-time algorithms that compute WEF-able allocations with an upper bound on the subsidy per agent in three distinct additive valuation scenarios: (1) general, (2) identical, and (3) binary.
elements.sent-2411-008: When all weights are equal, our bounds reduce to the bounds derived in the literature for the unweighted setting.
elements.sent-2411-009: The mathematical theory of fair item allocation among multiple agents has practical applications in scenarios like inheritance and partnership dissolutions.
elements.sent-2411-010: When agents have equal entitlements, as in inheritance cases, each agent naturally expects their allocated portion to be at least as good as others'.
elements.sent-2411-011: An allocation satisfying this requirement is called envy-free (EF).
elements.sent-2411-012: When the items available for allocation are all indivisible, an envy-free allocation might not exist.

elements.sent-2411-143: In this section, we expand the idea of fairness by considering weights and discuss the challenge of identifying WEF-able allocations, compared to EF-able allocations.

elements.algorithm-2411-001: [H] \caption{ \label{alg:general-additive} Weighted Sequence Protocol For Additive Valuations} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Instance $(N,M,v, \{w_i\}_{i\in N})$ with additive valuations. \Ensure WEF-able allocation $A = (A_{i})_{i\in N}$ with total required subsidy of at most $(W-1) V$. \State $A_{i} \gets \emptyset, \forall i\in N$ \State $t \gets 1$; $O_1 \gets M$ \State \texttt{$I \gets \{ i_1, ..., i_{w_i} \forall i \in N$\}} \While{$O_t \neq \emptyset$} \State \texttt{Compute a maximum-value matching $\mu^t = \{(i,\mu_i^t)\}_{i\in I}$ in $H[I,O_t]$} \State $A_i^t \gets \{\mu_{i_1}^t, ..., \mu_{i_{w_i}}^t\}$, for all $i\in N$ \State Set $A_i \gets A_i \cup A_i^t$, for all $i\in N$ \State \texttt{Set $O_{t+1} \gets O_t \backslash \cup_{i\in N}A_i^t$} \State $t \gets t + 1$ \EndWhile \State return $A$ \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-002: [H] \caption{ \label{alg:identical-additive} Weighted Sequence Protocol For Additive Identical Valuations} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Instance $(N,M,v, \{w_{i}\}_{i\in N})$ with additive identical valuations. \Ensure WEF-able allocation $A = (A_{i})_{i\in N}$ with total required subsidy of at most $(n-1) V$. \State Sort the items based on their value: $v(o_{1}) \geq v(o_{2}) \geq ... \geq v(o_{m})$ \State $A_{i} \gets \emptyset, \forall i\in N$ \For{$t = 1$ to $m$} \State $I^{t} \gets \argmin_{i\in N}\left(\frac{v(A_{i}^{t-1}\cup\{o_{t}\})}{w_i}\right)$ \label{selection-rule:identical-additive} \State $i^{t} \gets \max_{i\in I^{t}}\left(i\right)$ \label{selection role identical valuations} \State $A_{i^t}^{t} \gets A_{i^t}^{t-1} \cup \{o_{t}\}$ \State $A_{j}^{t} \gets A_{j}^{t-1}$, for all $j \in N \textbackslash \{i^t\}$ \EndFor \State return $A$ \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-003: [H] \caption{ \label{alg:binary-additive} Weighted Sequence Protocol For Additive Binary Valuations} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Instance $(N,M,\{v_{i}\}_{i\in N}, \{w_i\}_{i \in N})$ with additive binary valuations. \Ensure WEF-able allocation $A = (A_{i})_{i\in N}$ with total required subsidy of at most $\frac{W}{w_1} - 1$. \State $A_{i_{0}}^0 \gets M$, and $A_{i}^0 \gets \emptyset$ for each $i\in N$ \Comment{All items initially are unassigned} \State $U^1 \gets N$ \State $t \gets 1$ \While{$U^t \neq \emptyset$} \phase{Removing all agents without a transfer path starting from them} \For {$i \in U^t$} \State Find a transfer path starting at $i$ \If{ no such path exists} \State $U^{t} \gets U^{t} \setminus\{i\}$ \EndIf \EndFor \phase{Updating the resulting allocation} \State $I^t \gets \arg \max_{i \in U^t} \left( \frac{w_i}{v_i(A_i^{t-1}) + 1}\right)$ \Comment{Choose the agent who maximizes the gain function} \State $i^t \gets \max_{i\in S} \left( i \right)$ \Comment{Break ties using index} \State Find the shortest transfer path starting at $i^t$ \Comment{this path must exist, as $i^t$ would have been removed otherwise} \State Transfer the items along the path and update the allocation $A^t$ \State $U^{t+1} \gets U^t$ \State $t \gets t+1$ \EndWhile \State return $A^t$ \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-004: \caption{ \label{alg:general-additive} Weighted Sequence Protocol For Additive Valuations and Integer weights} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Instance $(N,M,v, \mathbf{w})$ with additive valuations. \Ensure WEF-able allocation $A$ with total required subsidy of at most $(W-w_1) V$. \State $A_{i} \gets \emptyset, \forall i\in N$ \State $t \gets 1$; $O_1 \gets M$ \While{$O_t \neq \emptyset$} Construct the flow network $G'=(V',E')$: \begin{itemize} \item define $V'= N \cup O_t \cup \{s,t\}$. \item Add arcs with the following properties: \begin{itemize} \item From $s$ to each agent $i\in N$ with cost $0$ and capacity $w_i$. \item From each agent $i\in N$ to each unallocated item $o \in O_t$, with cost $-v_i(o)$ and capacity 1. \item From each unallocated item $o\in O_t$ to $t$ with cost $0$ and capacity $1$. \end{itemize} \end{itemize} Compute an integral maximum flow of minimum cost on $G'$, resulting in the one-to-many matching $\{A_i^t\}_{i\in N}$\; \State Set $O_{t+1} \gets O_t \backslash \cup_{i\in N}A_i^t$ \State $t \gets t + 1$ \EndWhile \State return $A$ \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-006: \caption{ \label{alg:binary-additive} Weighted Sequence Protocol For Additive Binary Valuations} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Instance $(N,M,v, \mathbf{w})$ with additive binary valuations. \Ensure WEF-able allocation $A$ with total required subsidy of at most $\frac{W}{w_1} - 1$. \State $A_{i_{0}} \gets M$, and $A_{i}^0 \gets \emptyset$ for each $i\in N$ \Comment{All items initially are unassigned} \State $t \gets 1$ \State $R \gets N$ \While{$R \neq \emptyset$} \State Remove from $R$ all agents who do not have a transfer path starting from them \label{line:R} \State $i^t \gets \arg \max_{i \in R} \left( \frac{w_i}{v_i(A_i^{t-1}) + 1}\right)$ \Comment{ Choose the agent who maximizes the gain function } \State Find a transfer path starting at $i^t$ \Comment{ For example, one can use the BFS algorithm to find a shortest path from $i^t$ to $i_0$. } \State Transfer the items along the path and update the allocation $A^{t}$ \State $t\gets t+1$ \EndWhile \State return $A^t$ \end{algorithmic}
